question: Does the goal-anchored Prosoche context+decode policy (anchor-pinned + goal-relevance-ranked retention + drift pre-filtering + drift-triggered early-abort) solve tasks in FEWER tokens than a recency-chop / priority-packed baseline, at EQUAL-OR-BETTER task success, without becoming unable to adapt when the goal legitimately shifts?
constructs: tokens-per-solved-task (primary efficiency axis; lower is better)
task-success-rate (the guardrail — must NOT drop; forbids 'win by giving up early')
drift-rate (fraction of steps an independent judge labels off-goal; mechanism, not the claim)
goal-shift-success-rate (held-out subset whose correct answer REQUIRES re-anchoring; the anti-fixation guardrail)
primaryMetric: delta tokens-per-solved-task (Prosoche-anchored arm minus baseline), paired per task; negative = improvement
secondaryMetrics: per-arm tokens, turns, and tool-calls per task
cache-hit rate of the stable anchor prefix (mechanism evidence)
arms: recency-chop-baseline (the harness's historical join[-4000:] behaviour) — REQUIRES A REAL MODEL/AGENT; not run offline
priority-packed-baseline (agent.context_manager with no relevance_fn) — REQUIRES A REAL MODEL/AGENT
prosoche-anchored (anchor pinned+stable, relevance_fn=relevance_boost, drift pre-filter + early-abort) — REQUIRES A REAL MODEL/AGENT
primaryN: PENDING — the committed battery (prosoche-battery.json) is a routing-fidelity fixture only; a real run requires an external, decontaminated, drift-balanced task set sized so MDE <= 0.10 on the fractional-token-saving scale (see primaryMDE)

groundTruth.rule: each task's solved/unsolved label AND each step's on-goal/off-goal label must come from >= 2 INDEPENDENT judge families (judge != the answer agent, judge != the Prosoche gate), reporting inter-judge agreement (kappa >= 0.40 OR Gwet AC1 with CI)
groundTruth.use: GO/NO-GO — author-only labels (the current battery) do NOT satisfy this

taskSuccessGuardrail.rule: task-success rate on the Prosoche arm must NOT drop versus the strongest baseline by more than 0.02; a policy that 'saves tokens' by abandoning hard tasks is not a win — this is the whole ballgame
taskSuccessGuardrail.use: GO/NO-GO — token savings are claimable ONLY conditional on non-inferior success

antiFixationGuardrail.rule: on the held-out goal-shift subset (tasks whose correct answer requires re-anchoring), the Prosoche arm's success must NOT drop below the priority-packed baseline; focus must not buy efficiency with tunnel vision

safetyFloor.rule: no step that an independent judge labels safety/conscience-relevant may be pruned/aborted by the Prosoche policy as 'off-goal' (the 'attention is not blindness' property, tests/test_prosoche_safety.py); any such prune voids the run

noSelfJudging.rule: the Prosoche gate must not score its own ground truth; solved/on-goal labels come from independent judges, never from assess_attention

baselineContrast.rule: both baseline arms are scored on the IDENTICAL task set; the falsifier is that a baseline matches or beats the anchored arm on tokens-at-equal-success

decontam.rule: the external task set must pass tools/assert_decontam.py against the training/anchor corpus

inference.rule: primary delta reported with a 95% bootstrap CI excluding 0 AND a Robbins anytime-valid confidence sequence (the loop peeks/iterates)

claimCeiling: candidate_only; canClaimAGI:false; a pass may claim ONLY 'on this decontaminated battery the goal-anchored context policy solved tasks at N% fewer tokens with no measured success regression', nothing about the model 'being focused'
